headline: A US export-control directive forcing Anthropic to disable Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 dominates the cycle, alongside a wave of sub-1,000 tokens per second inference claims.
body: The recommended channels converge on the reported US government order requiring Anthropic to shut down Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all customers three days after launch, tied to export-control directives and a claimed jailbreak vulnerability [1][4], with Cyril Imhof framing the action as an EU access restriction [3] and IBM Technology covering the model's tier routing alongside an Apple-NVIDIA cloud deal at WWDC 2026 [6]. On speed, Better Stack details Xiaomi and TileRT's 1-trillion-parameter MiMo-V2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed hitting over 1,000 tokens per second via FP4 quantization, DFlash speculative decoding, and persistent GPU kernel runtime [0], and Google DeepMind's DiffusionGemma applying uniform state diffusion to shift local LLMs from memory-bound to compute-bound at similar speeds on H100s [2]. Julia Turc's interview with Groq founder Jonathan Ross covers the LPU's static scheduling and cost-per-token Pareto curves plus a strategic NVIDIA agreement [8]. Secondary channels read the Fable 5 ban as evidence of frontier-AI access control and social stratification [11][12], while CopilotKit's generative UI framework and IBM's S.A.T.A.N cybersecurity segment round out the building and safety threads [5][9].
